generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "First internal temperature sensor monitoring the styrofoam-insulated incubator box for real-time thermal data acquisition"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataStream1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Second internal temperature sensor monitoring the styrofoam-insulated incubator box for redundant thermal data acquisition and improved spatial coverage"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataStream2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "External ambient temperature sensor measuring surrounding conditions to provide environmental context for thermal model calibration"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureDataStream
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance InternalTemperatureDataStream1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]

instance InternalTemperatureDataStream2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance TwoParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Low-fidelity differential equation-based thermal model with two free parameters representing basic heat transfer dynamics in the incubator"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, PredictiveSimulationEnabler
]

instance FourParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "High-fidelity differential equation-based thermal model with four free parameters providing superior accuracy in predicting thermal inertia of the heatbed"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, PredictiveSimulationEnabler
]

instance RawTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unprocessed time-series temperature data from all sensors (internal and external) collected at high frequency for real-time analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataStream1, InternalTemperatureDataStream2, ExternalTemperatureDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreprocessingEnabler
]

instance CalibratedModelParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Optimized model parameters derived through calibration algorithms that align the digital twin models with real-world physical twin behavior"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalContextData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed environmental data including thermal gradients, ambient conditions, and historical patterns used for context-aware decision making"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalTemperatureDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FaultDetectionEnabler, OptimizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Central embedded controller interfacing with all sensors and actuators, aggregating data streams and executing low-level control logic"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Calibration framework employing optimization techniques (e.g., least-squares fitting) to adjust model parameters using real-time sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicCalibrationService
]

instance PredictiveSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engine executing dynamic thermal models to predict physical twin behavior under current or hypothetical conditions"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveSimulationService
]

instance FaultDetectionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Monitoring algorithm detecting deviations from optimal/safe operation through statistical analysis of sensor data and model predictions"
    DTDFVocab:enables SafetyValidationService
]

instance OptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Supervisory controller implementing model-predictive control and optimization algorithms to refine system parameters for improved efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationAndControlService
]

instance DataPreprocessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Stream processing pipeline filtering, aggregating, and normalizing raw sensor data before model ingestion"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationDataEnabler
]

instance ModelCalibrationDataEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data management component storing and versioning calibrated model parameters for historical analysis and model evolution"
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicCalibrationService
]

instance VisualizationDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization platform displaying real-time temperature profiles, control signals, and fault alerts using Grafana/Plotly"
    DTDFVocab:enables AdvancedVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous acquisition and aggregation of sensor data from the physical twin to track system state and performance metrics"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeTemperatureInsight
]

instance DynamicCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of mathematical models using real-time sensor data to maintain alignment between digital and physical twins"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

instance PredictiveSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Execution of simulations to replicate and predict physical twin behavior under current or hypothetical operating conditions"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveTemperatureInsight
]

instance SafetyValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integration of monitoring algorithms to assess whether the physical twin operates within safe and optimal boundaries"
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyComplianceInsight
]

instance OptimizationAndControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Use of supervisory controllers and optimizers to refine system parameters for improved efficiency and robustness"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationAction
]

instance AdvancedVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generation of interactive visualizations including time-series plots and 3D thermal maps for diagnostic analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualDiagnosticInsight
]

instance WhatIfAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Hypothetical scenario testing through predictive simulations to evaluate system response to potential disturbances"
    DTDFVocab:provides ScenarioAnalysisInsight
]

instance CorrectiveActionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated or human-triggered interventions to adjust system parameters when deviations from optimal operation are detected"
    DTDFVocab:provides CorrectiveAdjustmentAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeTemperatureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuously updated temperature profiles showing internal/external gradients and thermal distribution within the incubator"
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative metrics indicating the current fidelity between digital twin models and physical twin behavior through calibration validation"
]

instance PredictiveTemperatureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecasted temperature trajectories under current conditions or environmental scenarios with confidence intervals"
]

instance SafetyComplianceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time assessment of whether system parameters remain within predefined safety and performance boundaries"
]

instance VisualDiagnosticInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive visual representations of system state including thermal maps, control signals, and historical trends"
]

instance ScenarioAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Evaluated system responses to hypothetical disturbances or parameter changes through predictive simulations"
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated suggestions for adjusting heatbed power, fan speed, or other control parameters to optimize performance"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CorrectiveAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggered interventions to modify system parameters when safety or performance thresholds are violated"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatBedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary thermal actuator generating and supplying heat energy to maintain target temperature within the incubator"
]

instance TemperatureFanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Air circulation device ensuring uniform temperature distribution within the insulated container"
]

// C1: Physical Components
instance InsulatedContainer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Styrofoam box providing thermal insulation to minimize external environmental influences on the internal temperature"
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorSystem
]

instance RaspberryPiControlUnit : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Central embedded controller interfacing with all sensors and actuators via GPIO/ADC interfaces"
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorSystem
]

instance Environment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational context including ambient temperature variations and potential thermal disturbances"
]

instance IncubatorSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Complete cyber-physical system comprising physical components, sensors, and actuators"
    base:contains InsulatedContainer, HeatBedActuator, TemperatureFanActuator,
                InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2,
                ExternalTemperatureSensor, RaspberryPiControlUnit
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT exerts control over the Physical Twin (PT) via **command signals** generated by supervisory controllers or optimization algorithms, which adjust the **heatbed** and **fan** operations to regulate the internal temperature of the incubator. The control loop is closed through the **Raspberry Pi**, which executes commands to modulate heating power and airflow distribution. No explicit details are provided on **command types** (e.g., PWM signals for heatbed, duty cycles for fan) or **message formats**, but the system implies a **feedback-driven control architecture** where the DT monitors deviations (via sensor data) and triggers corrective actions. Conflict resolution mechanisms are not described, though the use of **calibration algorithms** suggests dynamic adaptation to mitigate discrepancies between the DT's predictions and PT's behavior. Command execution confirmation is implied by the closed-loop nature of the system, where the PT's response (temperature changes) is continuously validated against the DT's models."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator case study employs **real-time synchronization** between the Physical Twin (PT) and Digital Twin (DT) via **sensor data collection** (temperature sensors) at unspecified but likely **high-frequency intervals** (e.g., sub-second to second-level granularity) to ensure accurate model calibration and predictive control. The system utilizes **calibration algorithms** to update models dynamically, implying a **closed-loop temporal synchronization** where the DT continuously adjusts to the PT's state. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the **control loop of the Raspberry Pi-based temperature controller**, which must respond promptly to deviations in the PT's temperature (e.g., within milliseconds to seconds) to maintain stability. The temporal granularity for simulations and predictions is influenced by the **model abstraction level**: higher-fidelity models (e.g., 4-parameter thermal model) may require finer temporal discretization for accurate thermal inertia representation, while coarser models (e.g., 2-parameter) could tolerate broader time steps. No explicit synchronization frequency or latency thresholds are specified in the documents, but the use of **real-time monitoring** and **adaptive control** suggests low-latency, high-frequency data exchange is critical for operational integrity."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation for the incubator follows a **Cyber-Physical System (CPS)-based architecture**, integrating three core components in a **closed-loop orchestration**:
  1. **Physical Twin (PT)**: The styrofoam-insulated box with heatbed, fan, and sensors (acting as the plant).
  2. **Cyber Twin (CT)**: The Raspberry Pi-based controller and simulation models (handling data processing, calibration, and decision-making).
  3. **Environment**: External factors (e.g., ambient temperature) that influence PT behavior but are not directly controlled.
  The system employs **feedback-driven orchestration**: sensor data from the PT is fed into **calibration algorithms** (e.g., parameter estimation for thermal models), which update the DT’s predictive accuracy. The calibrated models inform **monitors** that evaluate system safety and optimality, triggering corrective actions (e.g., adjusting the heatbed or fan) via the Raspberry Pi’s controller. The architecture pattern is **model-centric**, with models serving as the bridge between data (sensors) and actions (actuators). Resource management is implicit, focusing on **computational efficiency** (e.g., balancing model fidelity vs. simulation speed) and **real-time constraints** (e.g., low-latency sensor-to-controller feedback). No explicit mention of cloud-edge hybrid deployment, microservices, or containerization is provided, but the use of a **single-board computer (Raspberry Pi)** suggests a resource-constrained, edge-centric implementation."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle for the described incubator case study includes the following phases: **1) System Design and Modeling**: The DT is built by integrating models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment, including both physical (e.g., styrofoam box, heatbed, fan, temperature sensors) and cyber components (e.g., Raspberry Pi controller). Models are developed at varying levels of abstraction, with calibration algorithms used to refine parameter estimates for accuracy. **2) Deployment and Integration**: The DT is deployed alongside the Physical Twin (PT) to enable real-time monitoring and simulation. Data from sensors (two internal and one external temperature sensors) is continuously collected and fed into the DT for model updates. **3) Operational Phase**: The DT operates in parallel with the PT, using calibration algorithms to update models dynamically based on sensor data. Monitors within the DT assess system performance for safety and optimality, triggering corrective actions (e.g., adjustments to the PT or algorithmic interventions) when deviations are detected. **4) Evolution and Maintenance**: The DT supports reconfigurability to adapt to changing environmental conditions, ensuring robustness. The lifecycle governance involves iterative model refinement, validation through experimental results (e.g., temperature regulation experiments), and alignment with the PT’s operational goals. Transition management between phases relies on calibration techniques and real-time data assimilation to maintain model fidelity and system performance. The lifecycle is governed by the DT’s ability to integrate models, data, and algorithms into a cohesive framework for continuous value addition to the PT."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model **fidelity** in the Digital Twin is determined by the **complexity and parameterization** of simulation models. The incubator case study demonstrates that **higher-fidelity models** (e.g., 4-parameter thermal model) better capture **thermal inertia** and other dynamic behaviors, improving predictive accuracy. **Validation and verification (V&V)** methods include **experimental comparison** between simulated and real-world PT behavior (e.g., testing the incubator’s temperature response under controlled conditions). **Uncertainty sources** arise from **sensor noise**, **model simplifications**, and **environmental variability** (e.g., ambient temperature fluctuations). Quantification involves **statistical error analysis** (e.g., mean squared error between predicted and observed temperatures) and **calibration algorithms** that adjust model parameters (e.g., thermal conductivity) to minimize discrepancies. **Error detection** is implicit in the DT’s monitoring layer, where deviations trigger **corrective actions** (e.g., recalibration or human intervention). **Quality assurance** processes include **iterative model refinement** (e.g., progressing from 2- to 4-parameter models) and **open-source validation** (e.g., the incubator’s reproducible hardware/software stack). The DT framework ensures **traceability** by linking model updates to specific data inputs and calibration events."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT's technical connection to the PT relies on a **local, embedded network** centered around the **Raspberry Pi**, which acts as the primary cyber-physical interface. The documents do not specify **communication protocols** (e.g., CAN bus, Ethernet, or wireless standards like Wi-Fi/Bluetooth), but the system's simplicity suggests a **low-latency, high-reliability** setup, likely using direct hardware connections (e.g., GPIO pins for sensors/actuators). **Bandwidth requirements** are minimal, given the limited data payload (temperature readings and control signals), but **latency** must be sufficiently low to enable real-time calibration and control. No explicit **redundancy mechanisms** (e.g., failover protocols) or **security protocols** (e.g., encryption, authentication) are mentioned, though the use of a dedicated controller implies basic robustness. The network topology is **star-like**, with the Raspberry Pi as the central node collecting sensor data and dispatching commands."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted on a **local, edge-computing platform** (the Raspberry Pi), which integrates the cyber and physical components of the incubator system. The deployment infrastructure requires **minimal computational resources** (sufficient to run simulation models and calibration algorithms) and **low storage capacity** (primarily for model parameters and sensor data logs). **Scalability mechanisms** are not addressed, as the system is designed for a single, small-scale use case. The deployment model is **on-premise**, with no mention of cloud or hybrid architectures. **Availability/reliability assurance** is implied by the closed-loop control system, where the DT continuously validates its models against PT behavior, but no explicit redundancy (e.g., backup controllers) or fault-tolerance strategies are documented. The open-source repository ([https://github.com/INTO-CPS-Association/example-incubator](https://github.com/INTO-CPS-Association/example-incubator)) suggests a modular, reproducible deployment approach, though no details on deployment automation or versioning are provided."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system for the incubator case study incorporates **safety-critical considerations** through its integration of **monitoring algorithms** that check for safe and optimal performance of the Physical Twin (PT). The system employs **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and environment, ensuring accurate parameter estimation. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are implied by the DT's ability to detect violations (e.g., deviations from safe operating conditions) and trigger corrective actions, either through human intervention or automated algorithms. However, the documents do not specify explicit **cybersecurity protocols**, **access control mechanisms**, **authentication systems**, or **threat mitigation strategies**. The DT framework relies on real-time **sensor data** (e.g., temperature sensors inside and outside the insulated container) and **model-based simulations** to predict and regulate system behavior, but no granular details on data encryption, compliance with regulations (e.g., GDPR), or consent management are provided. The **risk assessment** is indirectly addressed through the DT's role in predictive maintenance and fault detection, though no formal methodology (e.g., ISO 31000, NIST frameworks) is referenced."
]
